title: euclea elliptica
intro: Euclea elliptica, also known as the Blue Guarri or African Bluewood, is a deciduous tree native to tropical Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its bark and root showing antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and tannins. The medicinal properties of Euclea elliptica have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the bark and root of the Euclea elliptica tree are often dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
